findings.detail: The new payload guard does not actually scan all included payload text files. `TEXT_EXT` only accepts `.ts/.mjs/.js/.json/.md`, so payload files such as `req.config.json.sample` and `LICENSE` are skipped even though `req.config.json.sample` is explicitly listed in `package.json#files`. A future private handoff path added there would pass this CI guard while still shipping in npm payload. Scan all enumerated payload files as text, or explicitly include `.sample`/extensionless text files.

next_action: Fix the guard to scan every text payload file it enumerates, then rerun typecheck and the payload/unit verification.

observations.detail: `npm run typecheck` passed for the staged changes.
I could not run `npm pack --dry-run --json` in this read-only sandbox because npm attempted to create the isolated cache under `C:\WINDOWS\TEMP` and hit EPERM.
